delivery_notes: Physical USD delivery; standard correspondent banking settlement
No fixing lag — settles T+2 on standard basis
Most liquid G10 pair after EURUSD; tight bid-offer in spot and options

options.delta_label_convention: Delta labels (25DC, 25DP, 10DC, 10DP) refer to the BASE currency, GBP. 25DC = 25-delta GBP call (USD put); 25DP = 25-delta GBP put (USD call).
options.illiquidity_note: Deep and liquid across the curve; 1Y tradeable in size with tight spreads

notes: GBP rates (SONIA) modestly above USD rates (SOFR), giving mild positive carry for GBP long
Carry is small relative to EM pairs; forward points are slightly negative (GBP at forward discount)
BoE and Fed rate differential is the primary carry driver

market_structure.skew_driver: GBP downside tail risk (political, fiscal, current account); historically elevated post-Brexit
market_structure.liquidity_profile: Extremely liquid; second most traded G10 pair; institutional size executable with minimal market impact

risk_notes: UK fiscal credibility: Gilt market sensitivity can cause rapid GBP repricing (cf. Sep 2022 LDI crisis)
Current account deficit: UK runs a structural current account deficit, leaving GBP vulnerable to risk-off flows
BoE policy uncertainty: BoE credibility has been periodically challenged; surprises move GBP sharply
Commodity sensitivity: GBP has mild positive correlation with risk assets and commodity prices
Brexit legacy: reduced liquidity in some corporate hedging flows; ongoing trade friction uncertainty
